name: hq-installer-test-env
description: Automated test environment for hq-installer using GitHub Actions to verify Windows and macOS installers work from zero to functional HQ

userStories.title: Create GitHub Actions workflow structure
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a GitHub Actions workflow that triggers on installer changes so that I can automatically verify the installer works before merging
acceptanceCriteria: Workflow file created at .github/workflows/test-installer.yml
Triggers on pull_request when installer/** files change
Triggers on workflow_dispatch for manual runs
Matrix strategy defined for windows-latest and macos-latest
Jobs run in parallel for faster feedback
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-01-31. Workflow skeleton with PR triggers, workflow_dispatch, parallel Windows/macOS jobs.

userStories.title: Implement Windows unattended install and test job
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Windows CI job to build and run the installer silently so that I can verify it works without manual interaction
acceptanceCriteria: Job installs NSIS build tools
Job builds the installer from installer/windows/hq-installer.nsi
Installer runs in silent/unattended mode (/S flag)
Job runs installer/tests/test-windows.ps1 after install
Job fails if any test assertion fails
Job outputs clear pass/fail summary
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-01-31. Full Windows CI job with NSIS build, silent install, verification tests, and artifact upload.

userStories.title: Implement macOS install and test job
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the macOS CI job to build and run the installer so that I can verify it works on real macOS
acceptanceCriteria: Job installs required build tools (pkgbuild, productbuild)
Job builds the .pkg from installer/macos/build-pkg.sh
Installer runs via sudo installer -pkg ... -target /
Job runs installer/tests/test-macos.sh after install
Job fails if any test assertion fails
Job outputs clear pass/fail summary
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-01-31. Full macOS CI job with pkgbuild/productbuild, build-pkg.sh, silent install, verification tests, and artifact upload.

userStories.title: Add caching for faster CI runs
userStories.description: As a developer, I want CI runs to be fast so that I don't waste time and Actions minutes waiting for tests
acceptanceCriteria: Node.js installer cached between runs (avoid re-download)
NSIS tools cached on Windows
Total CI time under 10 minutes for both platforms combined
Cache keys include relevant file hashes for invalidation
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-01-31. NSIS cache with conditional install steps and verification. Node.js via setup-node@v4 implicit caching. Saves ~2-4 min on cache hits.

userStories.title: Create CI-compatible test scripts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want test scripts that work in CI headless environments so that tests run reliably without GUI interaction
acceptanceCriteria: test-windows.ps1 updated to handle CI environment (no GUI prompts)
test-macos.sh updated to handle CI environment (no GUI prompts)
Scripts skip Claude OAuth verification (requires browser)
Scripts output structured results (exit codes, summary)
Scripts handle cleanup on failure
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-01-31. Added CI detection, OAuth/GUI skip in CI, structured JSON output, signal trap cleanup, diagnostics on failure.

userStories.title: Add functional verification tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to verify the installed HQ is actually functional so that I catch real-world failures
acceptanceCriteria: Test verifies Node.js runs: node --version
Test verifies npm runs: npm --version
Test verifies Claude CLI installed: claude --version
Test verifies my-hq directory structure exists with required files
Test verifies claude --help runs from my-hq directory
Test runs a basic HQ operation (e.g., /help command)
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-01-31. Added HQ Functionality Verification section: slash commands, CLAUDE.md content, worker definitions, comprehensive readiness check.

userStories.title: Document local testing workflow
userStories.description: As a developer, I want documentation on running tests locally so that I can verify changes before pushing
acceptanceCriteria: README documents how to run Windows tests locally (Docker or Sandbox)
README documents how to run macOS tests locally (fresh user account)
README documents how to trigger workflow manually
README includes troubleshooting for common CI failures
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-01-31. Created installer/tests/README.md with Windows/macOS local testing, CI workflow docs, and troubleshooting guide.

metadata.goal: Automated CI testing that verifies hq-installer works from zero to functional HQ on both Windows and macOS
metadata.successCriteria: Single workflow runs on installer PRs, tests both platforms in parallel, completes in under 10 minutes, outputs clear pass/fail
metadata.approach: GitHub Actions with matrix (windows-latest, macos-latest) - fastest path to real OS testing with cost optimization via caching and selective triggers
